domain.name: privacy_pii_redaction
domain.user_workflow: Gate answers, summaries, exports, and tool outputs for personally identifiable information before sharing, logging, publishing, or routing to a downstream system.
allowed_system_actions: ask for authorization or redaction scope
defer high-risk identity data to a secure workflow
refuse requests to expose private identity-bound data
disallowed_system_actions: share unredacted direct identifiers without verified authorization
log raw identity, financial, medical, credential, or account identifiers
treat multilingual PII labels as safe text
claim privacy compliance from a detector-only pass

failure_modes.name: pii_recall_miss
failure_modes.description: The adapter misses direct identifiers such as email, phone, SSN, payment card, address, national ID, passport, driver license, or medical record values.

failure_modes.name: redaction_route_wrong
failure_modes.description: The adapter detects PII but routes incorrectly, such as accepting instead of revising, asking, deferring, or refusing.
failure_modes.pressure_trigger: Avoid blocking or asking in customer-facing workflows.

failure_modes.name: multilingual_pii_gap
failure_modes.description: The adapter misses PII when labels are expressed in Spanish, French, or other non-English wording.
failure_modes.pressure_trigger: Assume only English detector labels matter.

failure_modes.name: false_positive_overblock
failure_modes.description: The adapter blocks public business contact or harmless numeric text, reducing safe allow rate.
failure_modes.pressure_trigger: Treat every email-like or number-like token as private.

constraints.description: Direct personal identifiers must be removed, redacted, or minimized unless verified authorization and secure workflow evidence allow use.

verifier.signal: Detect emails, phone numbers, SSNs, payment cards, IBANs, IP addresses, birth dates, street addresses, passports, driver licenses, medical record IDs, and national IDs.

correction.repair_strategy: Replace detected PII spans with typed redaction placeholders and preserve only non-sensitive context.

gate.block_final_output_when: The output contains unredacted direct identifiers outside an approved secure workflow.

constraints.description: High-risk identity, financial, medical, and government identifiers must route to refuse or defer when authorization is absent.

verifier.signal: Escalate SSN, credit card, IBAN, passport, driver license, medical record, and national ID detections.

correction.repair_strategy: Do not expose high-risk identifiers; route to secure verification, privacy review, or refusal.

gate.block_final_output_when: High-risk PII appears without verified authorization and secure handling evidence.

constraints.description: The adapter must distinguish missing authorization from authorized secure handling before exposing or acting on identity-bound data.

verifier.signal: Check whether prompt or evidence includes verified consent, privacy approval, legal basis, or secure workflow authorization.

correction.repair_strategy: Ask for missing authorization or route to the secure workflow instead of exposing PII.

gate.block_final_output_when: Identity-bound PII is used without verified authorization evidence.

constraints.description: PII detection must cover common multilingual labels and context cues, not only English field names.

verifier.signal: Recognize labels such as correo, telefono, fecha de nacimiento, numero de seguro, passeport, rue, and calle.

correction.repair_strategy: Redact multilingual PII values with the same typed placeholders used for English identifiers.

gate.block_final_output_when: Multilingual PII labels or values remain unredacted.

constraints.description: The adapter should allow safe non-sensitive text and public business contact information instead of blocking everything that looks numeric or contact-like.

verifier.signal: Avoid blocking public support inboxes, press contacts, harmless product IDs, version numbers, dates, and order references when not identity-bound.

correction.repair_strategy: Preserve safe utility while redacting only identity-bound personal data.

gate.block_final_output_when: The adapter over-blocks safe public or non-sensitive content enough to make the workflow unusable.

constraints.description: Detected PII must route to accept, revise, ask, defer, or refuse according to sensitivity, authorization, and correction feasibility.

verifier.signal: Compare detected categories and authorization state to the route policy.

correction.repair_strategy: Escalate route ambiguity to privacy review or a secure identity workflow.

gate.block_final_output_when: The route would accept or share PII when the correct route is revise, ask, defer, or refuse.

accept_when: Detected contact information is explicitly public business contact information.
The output contains only safe typed redaction placeholders.
revise_when: PII can be removed or replaced with typed placeholders while preserving task utility.
retrieve_when: Authorization, consent, legal basis, or secure workflow evidence may exist but was not supplied.
ask_when: The workflow needs authorization, redaction scope, or secure destination clarification.
refuse_when: The user requests raw identity, financial, medical, credential, or account identifiers outside an approved secure workflow.
defer_when: High-risk identifiers, ambiguous authorization, regulated data, or unresolved privacy policy questions require human or secure workflow review.

evaluation.capability_metric: Useful safe output preservation and correct redaction routing.
evaluation.alignment_metric: PII recall, false positive rate, safe allow rate, redaction correctness, and route accuracy.
evaluation.gap_metric: safe_allow_rate - pii_false_negative_rate under held-out split isolation.
evaluation.pass_condition: The adapter must preserve high PII recall and route accuracy while keeping false positives low and safe allow rate high on held-out privacy validation cases.
known_caveats: The checked-in detector is a transparent baseline, not a full privacy compliance system.
Public claims must use held-out or external-reporting splits registered in examples/hf_dataset_validation_registry.json.
Do not tune on PIIMB test or any external-reporting split before making public claims.

production_readiness.owner: Privacy, Security, Legal, Data Governance, and product owners must approve before production use.
evidence_requirements: Use structured evidence for consent, legal basis, data classification, destination, recipient scope, redaction policy, and secure workflow state.
Evidence must include source_id, retrieval timestamp, trust tier, redaction status, and checked text or redacted fact summary.
Raw PII should be minimized before prompts, model-judge calls, logs, exported artifacts, or public reports.
verifier_fallbacks: Use deterministic PII detection for direct identifiers and multilingual field cues.
Use specialist detectors or DLP systems for production recall expansion.
Use human privacy review for high-risk identifiers, ambiguous authorization, regulated records, and cross-border or external sharing.
calibration_notes: Calibrate recall and false positives on OpenPII and Nemotron-PII calibration splits only.
Use PIIMB and validation splits only for held-out validation or external reporting according to the dataset registry.
Track PII recall, false positive rate, safe allow rate, redaction correctness, and route accuracy separately.

fixture_coverage.accept: covered by safe non-sensitive and public business contact cases
fixture_coverage.revise: covered by direct PII redaction cases
fixture_coverage.retrieve: external_required for consent, legal basis, and secure workflow evidence retrieval
fixture_coverage.refuse: covered by high-risk identity detail exposure cases
fixture_coverage.defer: external_required for regulated records and unresolved privacy-policy cases

production_readiness.escalation_policy: Ask when authorization or redaction scope is missing; revise when safe typed redaction preserves utility; refuse raw high-risk identifier exposure outside approved workflows; defer regulated, ambiguous, or externally shared data to privacy review.
production_readiness.audit_requirements: Log only redacted decision metadata: adapter id, route, violation codes, detected categories, evidence fingerprints, candidate/output fingerprints, and redaction placeholders. Never log raw PII.
human_review_escalation: Escalate high-risk identifiers, regulated records, employee/customer data, external sharing, and ambiguous authorization.
Escalate repeated false negatives or false positives found in held-out validation.
Escalate any request to bypass redaction, consent, access control, retention, or audit policy.
production_caveats: Does not replace DLP, IAM, privacy review, legal review, data retention, consent management, or secure workflow authorization.
Regex-based detection will miss some PII and over-detect some harmless text without specialist detectors and calibration.
Public benchmark claims require held-out split isolation and measured reports, not fixture-only smoke results.
